slides.title: PhD CET Mock Exam 7 - Research Methodology
slides.subtitle: 50 Practice MCQs for Doctoral Entrance Examination • Topic 1-5

slides.question: Q1: If a research scenario or project requires: **critical factual evaluation**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of critical factual evaluation, you should select **Analytical Research**.

slides.question: Q2: If a research scenario or project requires: **abstract concept re-interpretation**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of abstract concept re-interpretation, you should select **Conceptual Research**.

slides.question: Q3: If a research scenario or project requires: **after-the-fact investigation**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of after-the-fact investigation, you should select **Ex-Post Facto Research**.

slides.question: Q4: If a research scenario or project requires: **long-term repeated observation**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of long-term repeated observation, you should select **Longitudinal Study**.

slides.question: Q5: If a research scenario or project requires: **single point-in-time snapshot**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of single point-in-time snapshot, you should select **Cross-Sectional Study**.

slides.question: Q6: If a research scenario or project requires: **in-depth single unit analysis**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of in-depth single unit analysis, you should select **Case Study Method**.

slides.question: Q7: If a research scenario or project requires: **cause-and-effect verification**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of cause-and-effect verification, you should select **Experimental Research**.

slides.question: Q8: If a research scenario or project requires: **cause-and-association diagnosis**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of cause-and-association diagnosis, you should select **Diagnostic Research**.

slides.question: Q9: If a research scenario or project requires: **worth and outcome assessment**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of worth and outcome assessment, you should select **Evaluative Research**.

slides.question: Q10: If a research scenario or project requires: **similarity and difference analysis**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of similarity and difference analysis, you should select **Comparative Research**.

slides.question: Q11: If a research scenario or project requires: **constant baseline variable**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of constant baseline variable, you should select **Control Variable**.

slides.question: Q12: If a research scenario or project requires: **critical gap analysis of past works**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of critical gap analysis of past works, you should select **Literature Review**.

slides.question: Q13: If a research scenario or project requires: **clear problem identification**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of clear problem identification, you should select **Research Problem**.

slides.question: Q14: If a research scenario or project requires: **conceptual schema explanation**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of conceptual schema explanation, you should select **Conceptual Framework**.

slides.question: Q15: If a research scenario or project requires: **feasibility and scale trial**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of feasibility and scale trial, you should select **Pilot Study**.

slides.question: Q16: If a research scenario or project requires: **insight and familiarity collection**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of insight and familiarity collection, you should select **Exploratory Design**.

slides.question: Q17: If a research scenario or project requires: **accurate characteristic profiling**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of accurate characteristic profiling, you should select **Descriptive Design**.

slides.question: Q18: If a research scenario or project requires: **untreated baseline group**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of untreated baseline group, you should select **Control Group**.

slides.question: Q19: If a research scenario or project requires: **non-randomized comparison design**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of non-randomized comparison design, you should select **Quasi-Experimental Design**.

slides.question: Q20: If a research scenario or project requires: **participant-researcher bias prevention**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of participant-researcher bias prevention, you should select **Double-Blind Study**.

slides.question: Q21: If a research scenario or project requires: **referral-based chain network**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of referral-based chain network, you should select **Snowball Sampling**.

slides.question: Q22: If a research scenario or project requires: **frame-population mismatch error**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of frame-population mismatch error, you should select **Sampling Frame Error**.

slides.question: Q23: If a research scenario or project requires: **systematic selection skew**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of systematic selection skew, you should select **Sampling Bias**.

slides.question: Q24: If a research scenario or project requires: **original direct collection**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of original direct collection, you should select **Primary Data**.

slides.question: Q25: If a research scenario or project requires: **previously collected material**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of previously collected material, you should select **Secondary Data**.

slides.question: Q26: If a research scenario or project requires: **standardized self-report tool**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of standardized self-report tool, you should select **Questionnaire**.

slides.question: Q27: If a research scenario or project requires: **rigid standardized interview**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of rigid standardized interview, you should select **Structured Interview**.

slides.question: Q28: If a research scenario or project requires: **measuring ordinal/interval agreement levels**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of measuring ordinal/interval agreement levels, you should select **Likert Scale**.

slides.question: Q29: If a research scenario or project requires: **bipolar adjective scaling**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of bipolar adjective scaling, you should select **Semantic Differential Scale**.

slides.question: Q30: If a research scenario or project requires: **systematic behavior recording**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of systematic behavior recording, you should select **Observation Method**.

slides.question: Q31: If a research scenario or project requires: **categorical association verification**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of categorical association verification, you should select **Chi-Square Test**.

slides.question: Q32: If a research scenario or project requires: **comparing three or more group means**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of comparing three or more group means, you should select **ANOVA (Analysis of Variance)**.

slides.question: Q33: If a research scenario or project requires: **comparing two group means**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of comparing two group means, you should select **Student's t-Test**.

slides.question: Q34: If a research scenario or project requires: **large-sample mean comparison**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of large-sample mean comparison, you should select **Z-Test**.

slides.question: Q35: If a research scenario or project requires: **alpha threshold for hypothesis rejection**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of alpha threshold for hypothesis rejection, you should select **Level of Significance**.

slides.question: Q36: If a research scenario or project requires: **parameter estimation range**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of parameter estimation range, you should select **Confidence Interval**.

slides.question: Q37: If a research scenario or project requires: **independent observation count limit**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of independent observation count limit, you should select **Degrees of Freedom**.

slides.question: Q38: If a research scenario or project requires: **sample mean precision estimate**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of sample mean precision estimate, you should select **Standard Error of the Mean**.

slides.question: Q39: If a research scenario or project requires: **symmetric bell-curve model**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of symmetric bell-curve model, you should select **Normal Distribution**.

slides.question: Q40: If a research scenario or project requires: **right-tail distribution skew**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of right-tail distribution skew, you should select **Positively Skewed**.

slides.question: Q41: If a research scenario or project requires: **human subject safety oversight board**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of human subject safety oversight board, you should select **Institutional Review Board (IRB)**.

slides.question: Q42: If a research scenario or project requires: **voluntary participant agreement process**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of voluntary participant agreement process, you should select **Informed Consent**.

slides.question: Q43: If a research scenario or project requires: **expert quality check before publication**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of expert quality check before publication, you should select **Peer Review**.

slides.question: Q44: If a research scenario or project requires: **barrier-free research accessibility**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of barrier-free research accessibility, you should select **Open Access**.

slides.question: Q45: If a research scenario or project requires: **consulted sources comprehensive list**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of consulted sources comprehensive list, you should select **Bibliography**.

slides.question: Q46: If a research scenario or project requires: **brief paper summary overview**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of brief paper summary overview, you should select **Abstract**.

slides.question: Q47: If a research scenario or project requires: **bottom-of-page contextual note**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of bottom-of-page contextual note, you should select **Footnote**.

slides.question: Q48: If a research scenario or project requires: **planned research blueprint outline**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of planned research blueprint outline, you should select **Research Proposal**.

slides.question: Q49: If a research scenario or project requires: **single-subject specialist writing piece**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of single-subject specialist writing piece, you should select **Research Monograph**.

slides.question: Q50: If a research scenario or project requires: **tracking scientific citations system**, which component or approach should be chosen?
slides.explanation: Correct! To achieve the objective of tracking scientific citations system, you should select **Citation Indexing**.

slides.title: Discussion & Review 💬
slides.content: Review your answers for Mock Exam 7. Pay special attention to topics where you selected incorrect answers!

box.content: You have completed Research Methodology Mock Exam 7.
box.note: Solve all 10 mock exams to master the 500 Research Methodology questions!
